items.name: Ram Sita Wall Plate
items.description: Premium brass wall plate for hanging
items.lore: Images of Ram and Sita are kept in homes as a reminder of ideal dharma, devotion, and balance in relationships. Displaying them in living spaces is a way of keeping those values present in daily life.

items.name: 108 Jaap Metal Flower Plate
items.description: Sacred 108 bead japa mala for prayer
items.lore: The 108 beads represent the 108 Upanishads. Each bead chanted in Ram's name is said to invoke a blessing from the cosmic order that Ram himself upheld through dharma.

items.name: Dhoop Cone Holder
items.lore: Dhoop cones trace their origin to the yagnas of ancient Ayodhya. The rising smoke symbolizes prayers ascending to the heavens, carrying devotion from earth to the divine court.

items.name: Ram Naam Booklet
items.lore: Writing or reciting Ram's name is a long-standing devotional practice. Small booklets like this help devotees stay connected to that practice in everyday life.

items.name: Margdarshan Cards
items.description: Affirmation cards inspired from Ram's Margdarshan
items.lore: Inspired by values associated with Ram — such as duty, patience, and righteousness — these cards are meant to offer simple reflections and guidance for daily life.

items.name: Tulsi Mala Bracelet
items.lore: Tulsi is considered a manifestation of Goddess Lakshmi. In the Ramayana, Tulsi leaves were an essential offering during Ram's coronation ceremony in Ayodhya.

items.name: Sarayu Water Spray
items.description: Sacred Sarayu river at Ayodhya's boundary
items.lore: The Sarayu river flows through Ayodhya, where Lord Ram spent his earthly life. Its waters are believed to carry the blessings of millennia of devotion and are used in the most sacred rituals.

items.name: Ram's Favourite Chandan
items.lore: Chandan tilak on the forehead represents the third eye of wisdom. Lord Ram was always adorned with a sandalwood mark, symbolizing purity and the awakened mind.

items.name: Ayodhya Mitti Diya
items.lore: When Ram returned to Ayodhya after 14 years of exile, the entire city was lit with millions of diyas. This tradition became Diwali — the festival of lights. Each diya carries that homecoming joy.

items.name: Ram Fridge Magnet
items.lore: A miniature reminder of Ayodhya's Ram Mandir. Place it where you see it daily — a small anchor of faith in your everyday life.

items.name: Hanuman Chalisa
items.lore: The 40 verses composed by Tulsidas celebrate Hanuman — Ram's greatest devotee. Carrying the Hanuman Chalisa is believed to provide protection and courage in all endeavors.

items.name: Ayodhya Dhoop Sticks
items.description: Dhoop with the fragrance of Ram Temple
items.lore: Dhoop purifies the atmosphere and prepares the space for prayer. In ancient Ayodhya, specific blends were used during Ram's daily sandhya prayers at sunrise and sunset.
